UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores personal and medical information for a user of the Smart Paramedic app. This data is critical for emergency responses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

bloodType.description: The user's blood type (e.g., A+, O-, AB+).

chronicDiseases.description: A list of chronic diseases the user has.

medications.description: A list of medications the user is currently taking.

allergies.description: A list of allergies the user has.

homeLatitude.description: The latitude of the user's home location.

homeLongitude.description: The longitude of the user's home location.

EmergencyReport.title: EmergencyReport
EmergencyReport.description: Represents a single emergency incident reported by a user, including incident details and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EmergencyReport entity.

reporterId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated the emergency report. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N EmergencyReport)

reportTime.description: The date and time when the emergency report was created.

incidentLatitude.description: The latitude of the emergency incident location.

incidentLongitude.description: The longitude of the emergency incident location.

incidentAddress.description: A human-readable address string for the incident location.

incidentType.description: The categorized type of emergency (e.g., 'Accident', 'Fire', 'Fainting', 'Electric Shock', 'Bleeding').

status.description: The current status of the emergency report (e.g., 'Pending', 'Dispatched', 'EnRoute', 'Arrived', 'Resolved', 'Canceled').

receivingAgencyId.description: Reference to the EmergencyAgency assigned to handle this report. (Relationship: EmergencyAgency 1:N EmergencyReport)

dispatchedAmbulanceUnitId.description: Reference to the AmbulanceUnit dispatched to this incident. (Relationship: AmbulanceUnit 1:N EmergencyReport)

estimatedArrivalTime.description: The estimated time of arrival for the emergency unit.

resolutionTime.description: The date and time when the emergency incident was resolved.

EmergencyAgency.title: EmergencyAgency
EmergencyAgency.description: Represents an emergency response organization such as a hospital, civil defense, or traffic police.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EmergencyAgency entity.

name.description: The official name of the emergency agency (e.g., 'Al-Salam Hospital', 'Civil Defense Center 1').

address.description: The physical address of the emergency agency.

agencyLatitude.description: The latitude of the emergency agency's main location.

agencyLongitude.description: The longitude of the emergency agency's main location.

phoneNumber.description: The primary contact phone number for the agency.

email.description: The primary contact email for the agency.

AmbulanceUnit.title: AmbulanceUnit
AmbulanceUnit.description: Represents an emergency vehicle (e.g., ambulance) managed by an emergency agency.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AmbulanceUnit entity.

unitIdentifier.description: A unique identifier or name for the unit (e.g., 'AMB-001').

agencyId.description: Reference to the EmergencyAgency managing this unit. (Relationship: EmergencyAgency 1:N AmbulanceUnit)

driverId.description: Reference to the Driver currently assigned to this unit. (Relationship: Driver 1:1 AmbulanceUnit)

currentLatitude.description: The current latitude of the ambulance unit.

currentLongitude.description: The current longitude of the ambulance unit.

status.description: The operational status of the unit (e.g., 'Available', 'Dispatched', 'EnRoute', 'AtScene', 'Returning', 'OutOfService').

lastUpdateTime.description: Timestamp of the last status or location update.

Driver.title: Driver
Driver.description: Represents a driver or paramedic operating an emergency vehicle.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Driver entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the driver/paramedic.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the driver/paramedic.

email.description: The email address of the driver/paramedic.

agencyId.description: Reference to the EmergencyAgency the driver is affiliated with. (Relationship: EmergencyAgency 1:N Driver)

currentAmbulanceUnitId.description: Reference to the AmbulanceUnit the driver is currently assigned to. (Relationship: AmbulanceUnit 1:1 Driver)

status.description: The current availability status of the driver (e.g., 'Available', 'OnDuty', 'OnBreak', 'OffDuty').

FirstAidGuideline.title: FirstAidGuideline
FirstAidGuideline.description: Provides step-by-step first aid instructions for various emergency situations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FirstAidGuideline entity.

title.description: The title of the first aid guideline (e.g., 'Fainting', 'Bleeding').

description.description: A brief overview or introduction to the first aid topic.

sections.description: A list of instructional sections, each potentially with steps, images, and audio.

definition.description: Stores personal and medical information for a user. The `userId` path parameter must match `request.auth.uid` for read/write access, ensuring private ownership. The document's `id` field should match `userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: Records details of an emergency incident. Includes `reporterId` (user who created it) and `receivingAgencyId` for direct authorization. This denormalization enables `list` queries for both users (by `reporterId`) and agencies (by `receivingAgencyId`) without `get()` operations in security rules.

params.name: reportId
params.description: Unique identifier for the emergency report.

definition.description: Stores information about emergency response organizations. Includes a subcollection `members/{memberId}` (where `memberId` is `request.auth.uid`) to define roles (e.g., 'dispatcher', 'driver', 'admin') within the agency, ensuring authorization independence for agency-specific data access.

params.name: agencyId
params.description: Unique identifier for the emergency agency.

definition.description: Contains details and real-time status of an ambulance unit. `agencyId` field links it to the managing emergency agency, and `driverId` links it to the assigned driver, allowing for efficient authorization checks without cross-document `get()` calls.

params.name: unitId
params.description: Unique identifier for the ambulance unit.

definition.description: Stores profile and status information for a driver/paramedic. `agencyId` field links the driver to their affiliated emergency agency. The document's `id` field should match `driverId` (i.e., `request.auth.uid` for the driver managing their own profile).

params.name: driverId
params.description: Unique identifier for the driver/paramedic (should match their Firebase Auth UID).

definition.description: Publicly accessible collection of first aid instructions. All documents in this collection are globally readable.

params.name: guidelineId
params.description: Unique identifier for the first aid guideline.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure robust security and simplified debugging. Each collection is designed to encapsulate a homogeneous security posture, meaning all documents within a collection share similar access rules, eliminating the need for complex, conditional logic within security rules. Authorization relies solely on `request.auth.uid`.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
- **UserProfile:** Stored in `/users/{userId}`, where `userId` is the authenticated user's UID. This directly links the profile to the owner, ensuring access is strictly restricted to `request.auth.uid` for private data. The `id` field within the UserProfile document will mirror the `userId` from the path. This eliminates `get()` calls for ownership checks.
- **EmergencyReport:** Stored in `/emergency_reports/{reportId}`. To achieve authorization independence and support QAPs, the `reporterId` (the UID of the user who initiated the report) and `receivingAgencyId` (the ID of the agency assigned to the report) are both direct fields within the `EmergencyReport` document. This denormalization allows security rules to evaluate access based solely on the document's own fields, or by checking existence in a related membership collection (e.g., `/emergency_agencies/{agencyId}/members/{memberId}`), without needing `get()` operations on parent documents that could break atomic operations.
- **EmergencyAgency:** Stored in `/emergency_agencies/{agencyId}`. To manage agency-specific authorization, a subcollection `/emergency_agencies/{agencyId}/members/{memberId}` will store documents where `memberId` is a user's UID and the document contains their `role` (e.g., 'dispatcher', 'driver', 'admin'). This membership map is the canonical source for agency-specific roles. This provides a clear, denormalized structure for defining agency access.
- **AmbulanceUnit & Driver:** Stored in `/ambulance_units/{unitId}` and `/drivers/{driverId}` respectively. Both entities include an `agencyId` field, directly linking them to their managing `EmergencyAgency`. `AmbulanceUnit` also includes a `driverId`. This allows rules to grant access based on `resource.data.agencyId` for agency personnel or `resource.data.driverId == request.auth.uid` for individual drivers to manage their own unit/profile. This denormalization ensures access checks are self-contained within the document or a simple `exists()` check to the agency's members.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
- **UserProfile:** The path `/users/{userId}` explicitly secures individual user profiles. List operations on `/users` would be restricted to the authenticated user's own `userId` or not permitted, aligning with the
